commit.message:
feat: enhanced sidecar with signal polling (Phase 2 Unit 8) (#47)

* feat: enhanced sidecar with signal polling (Phase 2 Unit 8, Issue #38)

Extend the fleet-agent sidecar to handle context injection forwarding,
pause/resume/cancel signal polling, and redirect signal handling — the
capstone unit that ties together Units 1-7.

Server-side (Option A — RFC-aligned):
- Extend GET /agents/{id}/tasks/pending to include a `signals` array
  alongside pending tasks, avoiding a second endpoint and doubling poll
  traffic. Backward-compatible: existing sidecars ignore the new field.
- Add TaskService.get_pending_signals() to query signal events on active
  tasks and recently-terminated tasks (cancel/redirect).

Sidecar:
- New fleet_agent/signals.py: SignalPoller with shared-state architecture
  (asyncio.Event for pause/resume, flags for cancel, queue for context).
- Executor now interruptible: checks signals between each stdout line.
  Supports pause (blocks), resume (unblocks), cancel (terminates),
  redirect (terminates + new task on next poll), context injection
  (yields inline events).
- Poller integrates signal registration/unregistration per task lifecycle.
- Config adds fleet_signal_poll_interval (default 2s, shorter than task
  poll because signals are latency-sensitive control plane operations).

Tests: 38 new tests in test_signals.py covering all signal types,
deduplication, error handling, blocking behavior, and concurrent operation.
Updated test_pending_tasks.py to mock the new get_pending_signals method.

RFC 1 §7.2 items 5-7, §7.4 context injection flow, §7.5 pause/resume flow.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR #47 review — split signals, fix double-stream, type safety, memory leak

Blocker 1 (C1): Extract SignalState into fleet_agent/signal_state.py (138 lines).
signals.py reduced from 391 to 150 lines. State management (pause events,
cancel flags, context queues, redirect payloads, dedup set) now in SignalState
base class; SignalPoller inherits it and owns polling + dispatch + ack streaming.

Blocker 2 (C1 CRITICAL): Remove duplicate context_injected event stream.
Signal poller no longer emits its own context_injected ack — the executor is
the single source of truth (it emits the event when it actually processes the
context from the queue). _handle_context_injection is now synchronous and only
queues the payload.

Blocker 3 (F): Fix _single_event return type annotation. Changed from
`-> Any` to `-> AsyncGenerator[TaskEvent, None]` with proper import from
collections.abc.

Blocker 4 (D): Fix memory leak in _processed_signals. unregister_task() now
prunes entries from _processed_signals that match the task_id prefix
(composite key format: task_id:signal_type:timestamp). O(n) but only runs
on task completion. Added test_unregister_prunes_processed_signals.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: root <root@mlops-kelvin.marbell.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: Piotroski F-Score + Magic Formula + PEG — elite investor frameworks integrated

Backend (fundamental_scorer.py):
- _calculate_piotroski_fscore(): 9-criterion academic quality filter
  (Profitability×4 + Leverage/Liquidity×3 + Efficiency×2)
  Score 8-9 = STRONG, ≤2 = WEAK (value trap)
- _calculate_magic_formula_metrics(): Greenblatt EBIT/EV + ROIC + PEG (Lynch)
- New fields: piotroski_score, piotroski_label, ebit_ev_yield, roic_greenblatt, peg_ratio

Scoring (super_score_integrator.py):
- Piotroski bonus: +10pts (≥8), +5pts (=7), +2pts (=6), -8pts (≤2 = value trap)
- Magic Formula rank: computed across universe via EBIT/EV + ROIC combined rank
  Top 20% = +5pts, bottom 20% = -3pts

API (ticker_api.py):
- Exposes piotroski_score, piotroski_label, ebit_ev_yield, roic_greenblatt in analyze-ticker

Frontend:
- ThesisModal: F-Score chip (green/amber/red), MF Rank #N, PEG, ROIC chips
- TickerSearch: Piotroski F-Score row in Salud Financiera, ROIC + EBIT/EV in Fundamentales
- ValueOpportunity type: piotroski_score, piotroski_label, ebit_ev_yield, roic_greenblatt,
  magic_formula_rank, peg_ratio

Academic backing:
- Piotroski F-Score: +13.4% annual alpha (20-year study)
- Magic Formula (Greenblatt): 30.8% CAGR 1988-2004 vs 12.4% S&P 500
- PEG<1 (Lynch): GARP sweet spot for growth at reasonable price

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Add start_embedded_with() function accepting a configure closure for customizing AppStateBuilder before building

- Add collect_provider_configs() in zed prism provider to gather standard env vars (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, OPENAI_API_KEY, GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_API_KEY)

This enables Zed's embedded PrisM gateway to automatically discover provider API keys from standard environment variables, so users with existing Zed AI provider setup get working models in the embedded gateway without additional configuration.

commit.message:
feat(ouroboros): wire lower.hlx + emit.hlx for nodes 50-56

The bootstrap self-hosted compiler could parse axiomatic syntax (Phase 5)
but silently dropped node types 50-56 in lower_stmt with an error message.

lower.hlx:
- Added INST_TRY(310), INST_ENDTRY(311), INST_THROW(312) constants
- Added make_inst_try(), make_inst_endtry(), make_inst_throw() constructors
- Added lower_stmt cases for all 7 axiomatic node types:
  - 50 (try/catch): TRY + body + ENDTRY + skip-jump + catch block + patch
  - 51 (throw):     THROW with lowered value expression
  - 52 (intent):    GOVERN_CHECK tagged "intent" + body statements
  - 53 (for):       array_len + index register + LOOP + GET_ELEMENT + inc
  - 54 (while):     LOOP with re-evaluated condition + break/continue support
  - 55 (struct):    no-op (compile-time declaration, no runtime instructions)
  - 56 (extern):    lower the wrapped function statement normally

emit.hlx:
- Added INST_TRY/ENDTRY/THROW opcode constants (310-312)
- Added binary emission cases: TRY emits catch_pc u32, THROW emits value_reg u32

Closes the Ouroboros gap: HLX can now fully self-host axiomatic syntax
including exception handling, iterator loops, and intent declarations.

commit.message:
fix: make all migrations idempotent + complete migration journal

All 32 migrations (0000-0031) are now idempotent and registered in
the Drizzle journal. This enables CI/CD to run drizzle-kit migrate
on production where tables already exist from manual SQL execution.

Changes:
- 0001: ADD CONSTRAINT + CREATE POLICY wrapped in DO/EXCEPTION
- 0012: CREATE POLICY wrapped in DO/EXCEPTION
- 0015, 0019, 0020, 0027: ADD COLUMN → ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS
- 0023, 0024: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING
- _journal.json: added missing entries 0001-0029

commit.message:
feat: Sankirtan provider chamber using real MahaCellUnified substrate

Provider cells are REAL MahaCellUnified from steward-protocol:
- Prana from MAHA_QUANTUM (SSOT), not arbitrary numbers
- Integrity from COSMIC_FRAME, degrades on provider failure
- Headers pass parampara validation
- Source addresses SSOT-derived (MAHA_QUANTUM * N)

Priority: Google free (high prana) → Mistral free → DeepSeek paid (low prana)
Chamber selects by prana order, failover reduces integrity.
Daily reset refreshes all providers.

Includes Mistral adapter (OpenAI-compatible API) for free experiment tier.

commit.message:
feat: v1.0.47 — BI System (Business Intelligence) — Complete

Adicionado sistema completo de gestão de negócio (BI) para MUSICG:

ARQUIVOS CRIADOS:
- BI_README.md: Manual de implementação e setup rápido
- BI_EXECUTIVE_DASHBOARD.md: Template visual do painel executivo
- BI_DATA_WAREHOUSE_SCHEMA.md: Arquitetura DW (Star Schema com 14 tabelas)
- BI_SQL_QUERIES.md: 20 queries prontas para copiar/colar
- BI_METRICS_API.md: 12 endpoints REST para consumir métricas
- BI_GLOSSARY.md: Dicionário com 50+ métricas explicadas
- BI_LEIGO_GUIA_COMPLETO.txt: Guia em linguagem simples para leigos
- bi_dashboard.html: Dashboard visual HTML/CSS (cores MUSICG)

FUNCIONALIDADES:
✓ Rastreamento de receita (tips, tickets, ads, cursos, serviços)
✓ Análise de usuários (DAU, MAU, churn, crescimento, retenção)
✓ Monetização (ARPU, LTV, CAC, ROI)
✓ Marketplace & ingressos (contratos, validação, performance)
✓ Campanha de ads (CTR, conversões, ROAS)
✓ Alertas automáticos (crítico, atenção, sucesso)
✓ Análise geográfica (por cidade/estado)
✓ Dashboard responsivo com paleta de cores MUSICG

TECNOLOGIA:
- Database: PostgreSQL (Star Schema com 5 fact tables + 9 dimension tables)
- BI Tools: Metabase, Grafana, Tableau (compatível)
- API: 12 endpoints REST com JWT auth
- Frontend: HTML/CSS responsivo (mobile-friendly)

PRÓXIMOS PASSOS:
1. Programador cria database analítico (musicg_analytics)
2. Roda SQL schema de BI_DATA_WAREHOUSE_SCHEMA.md
3. Instala Metabase ou Grafana
4. Importa queries de BI_SQL_QUERIES.md
5. CEO/Founder acessa dashboard daily

Fix new arrivals being wiped on every sync

initDatabase() ran a destructive UPDATE clearing created_at for any
product older than 1 hour — called on every sync, this nuked new
arrivals after ~1 hour. Remove the stale one-time cleanup migration;
the isExistingStore check in syncProducts already handles first-sync
correctly (new stores get created_at = NULL on initial ingest).

commit.message:
Closes #24 — LifetimeSystem: decrement and destroy expired entities (#258)

Implement lifetimeSystem that decrements Lifetime.remaining by dt each
frame and destroys entities when remaining <= 0. Safe iteration via
collect-then-destroy pattern. Includes 11 tests covering exact zero,
negative, paused (dt=0), multi-entity, and property-based cases.
